reforms.description: The German government has implemented reforms to encourage entrepreneurship by reducing the discharge period for bankrupt entrepreneurs from six years to three years, provided they repay a portion of their debt. Additionally, to facilitate start-ups, the government plans to introduce a 'One-Stop-Shop' and a VAT exemption for the first years after starting a business. A legislation expanding the options for carrying forward tax losses was also adopted in 2016, aimed at facilitating the financial management of start-ups.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These reforms reduce financial risks and administrative burdens for entrepreneurs, thereby promoting business creation and growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reforms have been enacted and are currently in effect.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reforms represent significant changes in the legal framework supporting entrepreneurship.
reforms.source_quote: To facilitate start-ups, the government plans to introduce a 'One-Stop-Shop' and a VAT exemption for the first years after starting a business.

reforms.description: The German government plans to raise R&D spending from currently below 3.0% to 3.5% of GDP by 2025, with a focus on digitalisation and artificial intelligence. Additionally, an investment program was launched to support the expansion of childcare services until 2020, which indirectly supports R&D by improving workforce participation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing R&D funding is expected to enhance innovation capacity and productivity in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has publicly stated its intention to increase R&D spending by 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: The increase in R&D spending represents a major commitment to enhancing the innovation ecosystem.
reforms.source_quote: The federal government has launched an investment program until 2020 that supports expansion of childcare services.

reforms.description: Germany aims to roll out gigabit fixed broadband networks throughout the country by 2025 to enhance technology diffusion and support the adoption of data-intensive new technologies.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving broadband infrastructure is essential for facilitating innovation and the adoption of new technologies across sectors.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans for the broadband rollout by 2025.
reforms.importance_rationale: The initiative represents a significant investment in national infrastructure critical for future technological advancements.
reforms.source_quote: The government has set an ambitious goal to roll out gigabit fixed broadband networks throughout the country by 2025.

reforms.description: The German government has initiated a reform to enhance e-government services, mandating that all administrative services be available online within five years, which aims to simplify the process of starting a business and reduce administrative burdens.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Streamlining administrative processes through digitalization is expected to lower barriers for entrepreneurs and improve efficiency.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform has been passed into law but is not yet implemented.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards modernizing government services to support innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The Online Access Improvement Act stipulated that the central and local governments must offer their administrative services online within five years.

reforms.description: The coalition agreement in Germany envisages the introduction of R&D tax incentives to stimulate innovation, particularly targeting young firms and SMEs, which tend to respond more strongly to such incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: R&D tax incentives are designed to enhance innovation activities among young firms, thereby fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The coalition agreement outlines plans for the introduction of these incentives.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of R&D tax incentives represents a significant policy shift to support innovation in the private sector.
reforms.source_quote: The coalition agreement envisages the introduction of R&D tax incentives.

reforms.description: A new market segment for young high-growth firms was launched in Deutsche Börse in 2017, aimed at facilitating initial public offerings (IPOs) and providing technical assistance for compliance with IPO procedures.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Facilitating access to public markets for high-growth firms is expected to enhance their growth potential and attract investment.
reforms.status_evidence: The new market segment has been established and is operational.
reforms.importance_rationale: While significant, this reform is more of an adjustment to existing market structures rather than a major overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: A new market segment in Deutsche Börse for young high growth firms ('Scale') was launched in 2017.
